verbGlossary.INSPECTS FOR: {source} personnel conducts field inspections or audits to detect a specific {target} before it materializes (e.g., safety professional inspects for safety risk through jobsite walkthroughs)
verbGlossary.MONITORS: {source} tracks {target} metric on an ongoing basis to detect threshold breaches (e.g., safety professional monitors incident rate TRIR across the project)
verbGlossary.ENFORCES: {source} ensures that a required {target} response or mitigation action is carried out on the jobsite (e.g., safety professional enforces mitigation actions like fall protection compliance)
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIES: {source} discovers or documents {target} that persists after initial mitigation (e.g., QC inspector identifies residual risk during punch list walkthrough)
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source} activity directly improves {target} metric through systematic fieldwork (e.g., QC inspector drives risk mitigation completion rate by closing out deficiencies)
verbGlossary.VALIDATES AGAINST: {source} verifies installed systems against design intent to confirm {target} category is controlled (e.g., commissioning agent validates against design risk by testing MEP system performance)
verbGlossary.DETECTS: {source} uncovers {target} through cross-trade review or system-level evaluation (e.g., commissioning agent detects coordination risk during integrated systems testing)
verbGlossary.INTRODUCES: {source} or equipment brings {target} onto the project by its presence or operation (e.g., crane introduces safety risk through lifting operations near workers)
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} or material limits scheduling flexibility due to availability, lead time, or operational requirements (e.g., crane constrains schedule {target} when tower crane erection blocks other work)
verbGlossary.ELEVATES: {source} increases the level of {target} transfer requirement due to hazard exposure (e.g., ironworker at height elevates insurance requirement thresholds for the project)
verbGlossary.ENCOUNTERS: {source} or trade comes into direct contact with a site condition that activates {target} (e.g., earthmoving equipment encounters geotechnical risk when hitting unexpected soil conditions)
verbGlossary.DISTURBS: {source} activity disrupts site conditions in ways that trigger an environmental {target} (e.g., earthmoving equipment disturbs environmental risk through soil erosion or contamination exposure)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES: {source} or material creates vulnerability to {target} category through its nature or use on the jobsite (e.g., electrician exposes project to safety risk due to energized work)
verbGlossary.COMPOUNDS: {source} adds complexity to {target} by increasing the number of interdependencies that must be coordinated (e.g., electrician compounds coordination risk when rough-in conflicts with mechanical runs)
verbGlossary.SUBJECT TO: {source} is vulnerable to {target} category due to market conditions, sourcing constraints, or global supply factors (e.g., structural steel subject to supply chain risk from mill lead times and tariffs)
verbGlossary.DELAYS: {source} lead time or shortage directly pushes schedule dates and increases {target} (e.g., elevator materials delay schedule risk due to 16-plus week fabrication lead times)
verbGlossary.CONSUMES: {source} or equipment procurement absorbs schedule {target} time that was reserved for risk events (e.g., HVAC equipment consumes schedule buffer when delivery slips into planned float)
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} or material is regulated by permitting or code requirements that create compliance {target} (e.g., sprinkler fitter governed by regulatory and permit risk through fire marshal inspections)
verbGlossary.ASSESSES: {source} evaluates site conditions to quantify or characterize {target} before construction proceeds (e.g., surveyor assesses geotechnical risk by verifying grades and subsurface conditions)
